intro:
I couldn't find information on the plant "Ericoila squarrosa". However, based on the name, I suspect it may be a typo or incorrect name, possibly referring to the plant "Erica squarrosa" or "Erica carnea". Erica squarrosa is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae). 

If we consider Erica carnea, it is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe. 

Here's a paragraph about Erica carnea:

Erica carnea, also known as winter heath, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving circulation. The bioactive constituents of Erica carnea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenolic glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Erica carnea are teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
